Category Description: Covers all features exclusive to battery-electric (BEV) and plug-in hybrid (PHEV) powertrains: intelligent routing and range management, smart and bidirectional charging (V2G/V2H/V2L), battery health monitoring, and eco-driving coaching.

Parameter Description: Integrated EV route planning within the navigation system that calculates the optimal route accounting for real-time battery state, charger locations, charging speeds, and planned stops to ensure arrival within the vehicle's range.
Basic — Criterion: Static range estimate only; no live charger data or multi-stop optimisation.
Medium — Criterion: Connected routing with real-time charger availability and basic arrival SoC planning.
High — Criterion: Full multi-stop optimised EV routing: live charger status, dynamic arrival/departure SoC management, weather-adjusted range, in-route re-optimisation.
Optimised Parameter Name: EV Route Planner with Charging Stop Optimisation

Parameter Description: System that heats or cools the high-voltage battery to its optimal temperature window before a DC fast charging session, significantly increasing charging speed and protecting battery longevity.
Basic — Criterion: Manual activation only — driver must trigger preconditioning independently before DCFC.
Medium — Criterion: Automatic trigger via navigation destination set to a DC charger.
High — Criterion: Automatic nav-based preconditioning integrated with cabin pre-climatisation and departure timer.
Optimised Parameter Name: Battery Preconditioning for DC Fast Charging

Parameter Description: In-vehicle coaching system that analyses driving behaviour and/or forecasts energy consumption to help the driver maximise range and efficiency.
Basic — Criterion: Post-trip driving behaviour evaluation with efficiency score only.
Medium — Criterion: Real-time coaching tips and suggestions during the journey based on driving style.
High — Criterion: Predictive energy discharge forecast using planned route, topography, traffic, climate load, and driving style combined.
Optimised Parameter Name: Eco Driving Coaching and Energy Forecast

Parameter Description: Software-based mechanism allowing the owner to unlock additional motor output or performance modes via an Over-The-Air (OTA) purchase or subscription — without any physical hardware change. Enables the manufacturer to offer performance upgrades post-sale.
Basic — Criterion: One-time OTA unlock via dealer or brand portal (not self-service).
Medium — Criterion: OTA performance tier purchase available via in-car or mobile app store.
High — Criterion: Flexible subscription or on-demand OTA power unlock (e.g., per-day or per-event) purchased directly in-vehicle.

Parameter Description: System that automatically optimises the vehicle's coasting behaviour and energy regeneration level by anticipating upcoming events.
Basic — Criterion: Sensor-based only — radar/lidar distance to lead vehicle drives coasting optimisation.
Medium — Criterion: Sensor data plus map speed-limit anticipation for coasting management.
High — Criterion: Full predictive coasting using navigation: speed limits, curves, roundabouts, intersections, and topography.
Optimised Parameter Name: Predictive Energy Recuperation and Smart Coasting

Parameter: EV/PHEV Smart Charging (Cooperative / Managed Charging) (V1G Lev2)
Parameter Description: Capability to modulate the vehicle's AC charging rate in response to external grid signals, electricity prices, or operator commands (V1G — one-directional).
Basic — Criterion: Off-board V1G only — external EVSE or grid signal manages charging; no onboard intelligence.
Medium — Criterion: Hybrid off-board/onboard: external signals with some onboard scheduling capability.
High — Criterion: Full onboard V1G: dynamic tariff integration, grid-load response, and automated scheduling.

Parameter Description: Vehicle-to-Grid: bidirectional charging capability allowing the EV battery to export energy back to the public electricity grid, enabling demand response, grid stabilisation, and potential revenue generation for the owner via aggregator or energy-contract partnerships.
Basic — Criterion: Manual or scheduled V2G export only; no automated energy management.
Medium — Criterion: Automated V2G with basic energy-contract partner integration.
High — Criterion: Full V2G with revenue optimisation, aggregator integration, and automated grid-response capability.

Parameter: Vehicle to home energy V2H offgrid (blackout) and/or ongrid
Parameter Description: Vehicle-to-Home: bidirectional power export to supply a residential building.
Basic — Criterion: Backup power supply during grid blackouts only (off-grid mode).
Medium — Criterion: Scheduled on-grid V2H operation with manual control.
High — Criterion: Full HEMS integration with solar coordination, automatic grid/backup switching, and optimised self-consumption.

Parameter: V2L(Vehicle to Load - vehicle as a generator; Power Panel)  (Including V2V capabilities)
Parameter Description: Vehicle-to-Load: ability to power external electrical devices (tools, appliances, camping equipment) via onboard AC/DC sockets using the traction battery. Also covers Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) charging of another EV. Power output and socket variety determine the level.
Basic — Criterion: Single standard AC socket (≤2 kW output).
Medium — Criterion: Multiple sockets or higher power (2–3.6 kW) with integrated power panel.
High — Criterion: High-power output panel (>3.6 kW), multiple socket types, and V2V bidirectional charging capability.

Parameter Description: Automated authentication and payment initiation at a DC fast charger without driver action.
Basic — Criterion: No automated authentication; manual app/RFID required at public DCFC.
Medium — Criterion: Autocharge: proprietary or network-specific automated handshake for charging initiation.
High — Criterion: Plug & Charge per ISO 15118: cybersecure, cross-network interoperable automatic authentication and payment.

Parameter Description: Real-time visualisation of the DC charging session on the vehicle's displays: current SoC %, estimated time to target charge, charging power in kW, and session energy.
Basic — Criterion: Basic SoC % and estimated time remaining displayed.
Medium — Criterion: Detailed display: charging power (kW), session energy, and cost estimate.
High — Criterion: Full charging dashboard: power, SoC, cost, charger ID, session log, and predictive end-of-charge time on multiple screens.

Parameter Description: Ability to configure distinct charging profiles (target SoC, charging window, power limit) that activate automatically based on the vehicle's GPS location (e.g., different settings for home vs. work vs. public charging).
Basic — Criterion: Single preset charging schedule independent of location.
Medium — Criterion: Two preset profiles (e.g., home/work) with different schedules.
High — Criterion: Multiple geo-fenced location profiles each with individual power, SoC limit, duration, and tariff settings.

Parameter: EV/PHEV comfort charging (wireless inductive or conductive)
Parameter Description: Charging solution eliminating manual cable handling. Includes wireless inductive charging pads (park-and-charge) or automated robotic/conductive systems. Provides superior convenience, especially for daily home charging routines.
Basic — Criterion: Official OEM wireless charging pad accessory available (separate purchase).
Medium — Criterion: Factory-integrated wireless inductive charging pad (up to ~11 kW).
High — Criterion: Automated robotic/conductive or high-power (>11 kW) wireless charging — fully integrated from factory.

Parameter: Charge Coach (e-coaching recharge Inno; NMC batteries only)
Parameter Description: Specialised coaching system guiding the driver on optimal charging practices to extend battery life and efficiency — distinct from range coaching. Provides recommendations on AC vs. DC charging, ideal SoC windows for storage, and long-term degradation avoidance. Most effective for NMC battery chemistry.
Basic — Criterion: Basic static guidance on optimal SoC ranges for battery health.
Medium — Criterion: Dynamic coaching adapting AC vs DC recommendations to usage patterns.
High — Criterion: Personalised per-session coaching: charge speed, SoC targets, and long-term battery health tracking (NMC-specific).

Parameter: Variable charging control (set kW, time, max.SOC) down to SOC% and kW#
Parameter Description: Granular user control over the charging session.
Basic — Criterion: Start/stop current session only — no scheduling or power adjustment.
Medium — Criterion: Set target SoC % and schedule departure time; no granular power control.
High — Criterion: Full control: adjustable target SoC %, scheduled window, and adjustable charging power/current in defined steps.
Optimised Parameter Name: Variable Charging Control (Power, Schedule, Target SoC)

Parameter: Battery State of Health / Used Battery Certificate / Battery Passport
Parameter Description: Provision of transparent battery health data and certification. Includes State of Health (SoH) percentage, capacity degradation trend, and optionally a formal battery health certificate or EU Battery Passport for resale and regulatory compliance purposes.
Basic — Criterion: General battery health indicator or estimated capacity available in vehicle menu.
Medium — Criterion: Detailed SoH % with degradation trend accessible to the owner.
High — Criterion: Official battery health certificate and/or EU Battery Passport compliant digital passport — transferable to future buyer.
Optimised Parameter Name: Battery State of Health and Battery Passport

Category Description: All in-vehicle information, entertainment, and connectivity systems: navigation (embedded and connected), audio/video streaming, voice AI assistants, smartphone integration, in-car payments, and OTA services. This domain defines the digital experience of the vehicle and is one of the fastest-moving competitive battlegrounds — differentiating premium brands through interface quality, content ecosystem breadth, and AI-driven personalisation.

Parameter Description: In-vehicle turn-by-turn navigation system.
Basic — Criterion: Embedded system with offline maps and basic turn-by-turn routing.
Medium — Criterion: Connected navigation with occasional online updates and limited live services.
High — Criterion: Fully connected: real-time traffic, live POI search, cloud routing, and EV range overlay.

Parameter Description: Capability to update the navigation system's map database wirelessly over the air, ensuring accurate and current mapping without requiring a physical media update or dealership visit.
Basic — Criterion: Manual map updates via USB or dealer only.
Medium — Criterion: OTA map updates available but manually initiated by the driver.
High — Criterion: Automatic background OTA map updates — no driver action required.

Parameter Description: Live parking availability data integrated into the navigation system.
Basic — Criterion: Off-street parking availability only (car parks, silos).
Medium — Criterion: Off-street plus partial on-street occupancy data in selected areas.
High — Criterion: Combined off-street and probabilistic real-time on-street parking availability displayed on map.

Parameter Description: visualization of Map from satellite like with Google earth

Navigation guidance overlaid in real time on a live front-camera video feed, displaying directional arrows, street names, lane guidance, and POI markers. Can be rendered on the IVI central screen and/or in the instrument cluster for driver-axis visibility.
Basic — Criterion: AR overlay on IVI screen only (front camera + directional arrows).
Medium — Criterion: AR on IVI with lane-level arrow guidance and street name overlay.
High — Criterion: Full AR on IVI and driver-axis cluster/HUD with dynamic arrows, speed limit display, and hazard highlights.

Parameter Description: Display of the navigation map (beyond a simple route ribbon) within the digital instrument cluster, giving the driver a full spatial view of the road ahead and surrounding context without looking away from the driving axis.
Basic — Criterion: Route ribbon or simple arrow guidance in cluster only.
Medium — Criterion: Partial map view in cluster (small window format).
High — Criterion: Full navigation map natively rendered in the digital cluster.
Optimised Parameter Name: Navigation Map Display in Instrument Cluster

Parameter: Trailer/Tow Specific  Navigation (low clearance alert included)
Parameter Description: Navigation mode specifically designed for when the vehicle is towing a trailer. This typically includes considerations for vehicle dimensions, weight, and potential restrictions (e.g., low clearance alerts, routes avoiding sharp turns or steep grades).
Dashcam functionality using on-board cameras.
Basic — Criterion: Official OEM dashcam accessory available (external, sold separately).
Medium — Criterion: Semi-integrated dashcam using dedicated OEM hardware with in-car storage/viewing.
High — Criterion: Fully integrated: existing onboard cameras used as dashcam, footage stored to internal memory or USB with in-car review.

Parameter Description: An in-vehicle application store embedded in the infotainment system, enabling drivers to browse, download, and manage third-party apps (navigation, streaming, productivity) directly on the head unit.
Basic — Criterion: Pre-loaded curated apps only — no user-downloadable store.
Medium — Criterion: Basic OEM app store with limited downloadable catalogue.
High — Criterion: Full open/semi-open app store with regular catalogue updates, ratings, and OTA app installation.

Parameter Description: Radio system combining traditional broadcast reception (FM, DAB+) with internet-based IP radio streaming, automatically or manually switching between sources to maintain uninterrupted audio when broadcast signal quality drops.
Basic — Criterion: FM + DAB digital radio only — no internet streaming fallback.
Medium — Criterion: FM/DAB with manual internet radio option via connected infotainment.
High — Criterion: Hybrid radio with seamless automatic switching between FM/DAB broadcast and IP streaming.

Parameter: Music streaming : Spotify/ Apple Music/Amazon Music
Parameter Description: Native integration of major music streaming platforms (Spotify, Apple Music, Amazon Music, etc.) directly within the infotainment system, accessible without requiring a connected smartphone.
Basic — Criterion: Streaming available via CarPlay/Android Auto only (smartphone-dependent).
Medium — Criterion: Native embedded streaming app with linked account, requires hotspot data.
High — Criterion: Native embedded streaming with built-in SIM/embedded data — no smartphone required.

Parameter Description: Native integration of video streaming services (YouTube, Netflix, Amazon Prime Video, etc.) on the infotainment display, typically restricted to parked-vehicle use or rear-seat screens.
Basic — Criterion: Video streaming via mirrored smartphone only (CarPlay/AA).
Medium — Criterion: One video streaming app natively embedded (parked use only).
High — Criterion: Multiple video streaming services natively embedded with optional rear-seat access (parked only).

Parameter Description: Full internet browser embedded in the infotainment system, providing unrestricted web access (typically when parked). Higher levels support modern web standards and may allow passenger-side browsing while in motion.
Basic — Criterion: Basic embedded browser with limited functionality (parked only).
Medium — Criterion: Full-featured embedded browser supporting modern web standards (parked only).
High — Criterion: Full browser with optional passenger use while driving and embedded data connectivity.

Parameter: Gaming  (Headunit= medium); (immersive/St Wheel / Pedals = high) Car as a gamepad
Parameter Description: In-cabin gaming capability.
Basic — Criterion: Smartphone/device mirroring for gaming on the head unit screen.
Medium — Criterion: Games playable natively on the vehicle's head unit (touch/controller input).
High — Criterion: Immersive gaming using vehicle controls (steering wheel, pedals) or dedicated controllers with advanced graphics.

Parameter: Smart Device Screen Casting (i.e. Chromecast, Airplay , Miracast etc…)
Parameter Description: Direct wireless screen mirroring or casting from smartphones and tablets to the infotainment display, using consumer casting standards such as Chromecast, AirPlay, or Miracast — beyond the scope of CarPlay/Android Auto.
Basic — Criterion: Wired screen mirroring via USB only.
Medium — Criterion: Wireless casting for one platform (e.g., Chromecast OR AirPlay, not both).
High — Criterion: Wireless casting supporting multiple simultaneous protocols (Chromecast, AirPlay, Miracast).

Parameter: Wireless Mirroring Carplay & Android Auto (Only One wireless M: 12,Both wireless H :14)
Parameter Description: Wireless smartphone projection via Apple CarPlay and/or Android Auto. Medium = one platform wireless only; High = both CarPlay and Android Auto supported wirelessly simultaneously.
Basic — Criterion: Both CarPlay and Android Auto via wired connection only.
Medium — Criterion: One of the two (CarPlay or Android Auto) supported wirelessly; the other is wired.
High — Criterion: Both Apple CarPlay and Android Auto supported fully wirelessly.
Optimised Parameter Name: Wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto

Parameter: Branded premium audio ( Mid<12 spk, High =>12 spk )
Parameter Description: Factory-fitted premium audio system by a recognised brand (e.g., Bose, Harman Kardon, Meridian, Bang & Olufsen).
Basic — Criterion: Standard (non-branded) audio system.
Medium — Criterion: Branded premium audio system with fewer than 12 speakers.
High — Criterion: Branded premium audio system with 12 or more speakers.

Parameter Description: Advanced audio system delivering a three-dimensional soundstage through height channels or object-based spatial audio processing, creating an immersive listening experience beyond standard surround sound.
Basic — Criterion: Standard stereo or basic surround audio.
Medium — Criterion: Multi-channel surround sound without height channels.
High — Criterion: 3D/immersive sound with dedicated height channels and spatial audio processing.

Parameter Description: Premium audio enhanced with haptic/tactile transducers integrated into the seats, synchronised to bass content and audio events to create a full-body immersive listening experience.
Basic — Criterion: Standard audio — no haptic/tactile integration.
Medium — Criterion: Seat-mounted tactile transducers for bass enhancement only.
High — Criterion: Full 4D audio with seat vibrators synchronised to audio content for whole-body immersion.
Optimised Parameter Name: Premium 4D Audio System with Seat Haptics

Parameter Description: Audio system certified for Dolby Atmos playback — the industry-leading object-based surround-sound format — delivering cinema-quality spatial audio within the vehicle cabin.
Basic — Criterion: Standard multi-channel surround sound.
Medium — Criterion: Object-based audio processing without official Dolby Atmos certification.
High — Criterion: Officially certified Dolby Atmos playback system.

Parameter Description: Speakers integrated into the seat headrests to create personalised audio zones for individual occupants, enabling discrete navigation prompts, phone calls, or tailored sound experiences per seat position.
Basic — Criterion: Standard cabin audio only — no headrest speakers.
Medium — Criterion: Headrest speakers for navigation or phone calls in front row.
High — Criterion: Individual audio zones via headrest speakers for all occupants with personalised sound per seat.

Parameter Description: Active Noise Cancellation (ANC) system using microphones and the audio system's speakers to generate anti-phase sound waves that specifically counteract and reduce engine noise perceived inside the cabin.
Basic — Criterion: No active engine noise cancellation — passive insulation only.
Medium — Criterion: ANC targeting primary engine frequency band.
High — Criterion: Broadband ANC targeting multiple engine harmonics across all RPM ranges.

Parameter Description: Active Road Noise Cancellation (RNC) system that detects and cancels tyre and road surface noise transmitted through the vehicle structure into the cabin, improving ride refinement.
Basic — Criterion: No active road noise cancellation.
Medium — Criterion: RNC targeting primary tyre/road resonance frequencies.
High — Criterion: Broadband RNC with adaptive tuning for different road surfaces and speeds.

Parameter Description: Active Wind Noise Cancellation system targeting aerodynamic and wind buffeting noise at higher speeds, complementing passive sealing measures for a quieter cabin at motorway speeds.
Basic — Criterion: No active wind noise cancellation.
Medium — Criterion: Wind noise reduction at specific resonance frequencies.
High — Criterion: Adaptive wind noise cancellation active across speed range with real-time adjustment.

Parameter Description: Artificially generated interior sound design for EVs, providing acoustic feedback linked to throttle, speed, and drive mode to enrich the driving experience and compensate for the absence of engine sound.
Basic — Criterion: Fixed artificial internal sound independent of driving mode.
Medium — Criterion: Internal sound varying by drive mode (Eco, Normal, Sport).
High — Criterion: Fully adaptive sound linked to acceleration, speed, torque, and drive mode — with user customisation options.

Parameter Description: Distinctive or emotional artificial sound projected externally by an EV, which is *in addition to* or a *stylized version of* the mandatory Acoustic Vehicle Alerting System (AVAS) sound. Standard, compliant AVAS alone is 'No' for this feature. This feature is classified 'High' if any such distinct, additional or uniquely branded external sound design (standard or optional) is present.
Embedded cellular modem providing an in-car Wi-Fi hotspot for passengers.
Basic — Criterion: No integrated Wi-Fi hotspot — phone tethering only.
Medium — Criterion: 4G LTE in-car Wi-Fi hotspot (embedded or SIM-based).
High — Criterion: 5G in-car Wi-Fi hotspot with high-bandwidth data plan.

Parameter Description: Voice-activated assistant for hands-free control of vehicle systems, navigation, and infotainment.
Basic — Criterion: Cloud-connected voice recognition for basic vehicle and infotainment commands.
Medium — Criterion: Advanced conversational AI with LLM integration (e.g., ChatGPT) for complex queries.
High — Criterion: Proactive AI assistant that learns habits, provides predictive suggestions, and manages commands across all vehicle domains.

Parameter: Connected Profiles (with all parameters music, adas, comfort)
Parameter Description: Cloud-synchronised personalisation profiles storing and automatically applying a comprehensive set of user preferences — seating, mirrors, climate, ADAS sensitivity, favourite destinations, audio settings — whenever a recognised driver enters the vehicle.
Basic — Criterion: Local driver profiles stored on vehicle only (seat, mirrors, favourites).
Medium — Criterion: Cloud-synced profiles covering key preferences (seat, climate, audio) accessible across vehicles.
High — Criterion: Full cloud-synced profiles covering all domains: seating, ADAS, navigation, infotainment, music, and payment credentials.

Parameter Description: Embedded digital payment capability within the infotainment system allowing the driver to pay for services — EV charging, parking, tolls, fuel, subscriptions — directly from the vehicle without a physical card or separate device.
Basic — Criterion: Linked payment card for EV charging only.
Medium — Criterion: In-car payment for charging and parking via integrated wallet.
High — Criterion: Full in-car payment gateway: charging, parking, tolls, on-demand subscriptions, and in-car marketplace.

Parameter: Biometric recognition for personalization and buy (fingerprint/face/voice)
Parameter Description: Use of fingerprint, facial, or voice biometrics for secure driver identification, enabling automatic profile loading and authorising in-car purchases without a PIN or password.
Basic — Criterion: Single biometric modality for driver identification only.
Medium — Criterion: Biometric ID used for profile personalisation and basic in-car authentication.
High — Criterion: Multi-modal biometrics (fingerprint + face + voice) for personalisation, payment authorisation, and access control.

Parameter Description: Video conferencing applications (Microsoft Teams, Google Meet, Zoom, Webex) accessible on the infotainment screen when parked.
Basic — Criterion: Audio-only conferencing via integrated speaker system (parked).
Medium — Criterion: At least one major video conferencing app available on infotainment (parked).
High — Criterion: Video conferencing using embedded interior camera, microphone array, and speaker system (parked).

Parameter Description: A digital, searchable version of the vehicle owner's manual embedded in the infotainment system, enabling drivers to quickly find operating instructions and safety information contextually — without physical paperwork.
Basic — Criterion: Static PDF or QR-code link to online manual only.
Medium — Criterion: Interactive searchable digital handbook within the infotainment system.
High — Criterion: Context-aware handbook proactively surfacing relevant information based on active vehicle function or driver query.

Parameter Description: Dedicated infotainment pages displaying real-time off-road or performance telemetry: pitch/roll angles, wheel articulation, terrain mode status, traction indicators, G-force meter, and lap timers.
Basic — Criterion: Basic telemetry page (pitch/roll angles, compass).
Medium — Criterion: Extended off-road page with traction data, differential status, and drive-mode indicator.
High — Criterion: Full suite: real-time angle, traction, terrain mode, driveline status, G-force meter, configurable layout.

Parameter: Drive mode selection (terrain mode, sport mode, race…) Basic: predefined settings; High: individual drive mode
Parameter Description: System allowing the driver to select or configure driving modes that alter powertrain, steering, suspension, traction, and braking parameters.
Basic — Criterion: Fixed OEM-defined presets only (Eco, Normal, Sport).
Medium — Criterion: Predefined modes plus ability to adjust 1–2 parameters (e.g., throttle response, steering weight).
High — Criterion: Fully customisable individual mode: driver sets and saves all parameters (powertrain, steering, suspension, regen, HMI theme).

Parameter Description: Dedicated infotainment application consolidating all trailer-related information and controls into a single interface: trailer profiles, hitch guidance, real-time trailer telemetry, lighting status, and camera feeds.
Basic — Criterion: Basic towing info page (tongue weight, trailer connection status).
Medium — Criterion: Towing assistant with trailer profile setup and key real-time data.
High — Criterion: Full trailer management hub: profiles, real-time telemetry, lighting status, camera feeds, and towing guidance in one interface.

Category Description: Features accessible via the manufacturer's companion smartphone application, enabling owners to monitor and control their vehicle remotely. Spans vehicle status monitoring (doors, windows, range), remote commands (lock/unlock, climate pre-conditioning), digital key access, and advanced telematics. Increasingly important for fleet managers and multi-driver households, and a key retention driver for connected-services subscriptions.

Parameter Description: Ability to view door lock status (locked/unlocked) via a companion mobile application.
Companion app feature showing the open/closed/partially-open status of each vehicle window, helping prevent weather damage when windows are inadvertently left open.
Basic — Criterion: Binary open/closed indication for all windows.
Medium — Criterion: Percentage open level shown for each individual window.
High — Criterion: Individual window status with alert notification if left open.

Parameter Description: Ability to view the current status of the vehicle's headlights (e.g., on, off, auto mode) via a companion mobile application.
Ability to view the current status of the vehicle's trunk/tailgate (e.g., open, closed) via a companion mobile application.
Optimised Parameter Name: Remote Trunk and Tailgate Status Monitoring

Parameter Description: Companion app display of the vehicle's current fuel level (ICE/hybrid) or battery SoC and estimated remaining driving range (EV/PHEV), helping owners plan refuelling or recharging needs.
Basic — Criterion: Current fuel level or battery SoC % displayed.
Medium — Criterion: Estimated remaining driving range displayed.
High — Criterion: Range with contextual guidance (nearby chargers/stations if range is low) and historical range trends.

Parameter Description: Companion app feature that displays the vehicle's last known GPS location on a map, helping drivers locate their parked vehicle in unfamiliar areas or large car parks.
Basic — Criterion: Last known GPS coordinates displayed as text.
Medium — Criterion: Interactive map showing parked vehicle location.
High — Criterion: Map with vehicle position, nearby landmarks, and walking navigation to vehicle.

Parameter Description: Companion app access to historical trip data including distance, duration, energy or fuel consumption, average speed, driving efficiency scores, and CO₂ emissions — enabling drivers to track and improve their driving habits.
Basic — Criterion: Distance and duration of last trip only.
Medium — Criterion: Multi-trip history with energy/fuel consumption and average speed.
High — Criterion: Detailed analytics dashboard: driving style score, CO₂ savings, efficiency trends, exportable data.
Optimised Parameter Name: Remote Trip History and Driving Analytics

Parameter Description: Notifications or reminders regarding upcoming scheduled vehicle maintenance (e.g., service intervals, inspections), accessible via the companion mobile app and/or displayed in the vehicle.
Optimised Parameter Name: Remote Maintenance Reminder and Service Alerts

Parameter Description: Ability to view an overview of the vehicle's health status (e.g., tire pressure information, status of critical fluid levels, diagnostic trouble codes, or general system check results) via a companion mobile application.
Remote command via the companion app to activate the vehicle's headlights (typically as a locator flash), helping the driver find the vehicle in a crowded or dark parking area.
Basic — Criterion: Remote light flash to locate vehicle.
Medium — Criterion: Remote headlight on/off with duration control.
High — Criterion: Remote light activation with welcome/farewell light sequence.

Parameter Description: Ability to remotely lock or unlock the vehicle's doors via a companion mobile application.
Remote activation of the vehicle's horn and/or hazard indicators via the companion app, used to locate the car or as an emergency alert.
Basic — Criterion: Remote hazard light activation only.
Medium — Criterion: Remote horn and hazard light activation together.
High — Criterion: Remote horn, hazard, and personalised light/sound sequence with location sharing.
Optimised Parameter Name: Remote Horn and Hazard Light Activation

Parameter Description: Remote command via the companion app to unlock or open the boot/trunk independently, without unlocking the main passenger doors — useful for secure parcel delivery or valet access.
Basic — Criterion: Remote trunk unlock independent of door lock state.
Medium — Criterion: Remote trunk unlock with open/close command (powered tailgate).
High — Criterion: Remote trunk unlock plus scheduled access window for delivery services.

Parameter Description: Remote command via the companion app to open or close the vehicle's windows and/or sunroof, useful for ventilation management or confirming closure before departing.
Basic — Criterion: Remote full open or full close of windows.
Medium — Criterion: Remote window operation with partial position adjustment.
High — Criterion: Remote window and sunroof control with comfort vent preset and scheduled ventilation.

Parameter Description: Remote activation of the vehicle's climate control system via the companion app, allowing the cabin to reach a desired temperature before the driver enters — particularly valuable in extreme weather or for EV pre-conditioning.
Basic — Criterion: Remote climate on/off at last-used temperature setting.
Medium — Criterion: Remote climate with target temperature setting and departure timer.
High — Criterion: Fully customisable: zone-by-zone temperature, seat heating/cooling, scheduled multi-event timers.

Parameter: Digital Key ( Access/start from Smart Device) NFC, UWB, BT
Parameter Description: Technology enabling a compatible smartphone to function as a vehicle key.
Basic — Criterion: NFC-based digital key requiring active device tap to unlock and start.
Medium — Criterion: BLE digital key with proximity unlock and active start confirmation.
High — Criterion: UWB passive digital key: hands-free proximity unlock and authenticated start without any device interaction.
Optimised Parameter Name: Digital Key via Smartphone (NFC / Bluetooth / UWB)

Parameter Description: Remote engine start via the companion app for ICE or hybrid vehicles, primarily to pre-condition the cabin climate before the driver boards. Subject to legal requirements by market.
Basic — Criterion: Remote engine start via physical key fob only (not app-based).
Medium — Criterion: Remote engine start via app with timer scheduling.
High — Criterion: Remote start with full cabin pre-conditioning (climate, seat heat/cool) and status feedback in app.
Optimised Parameter Name: Remote Engine Start (ICE and Hybrid Vehicles)

Parameter Description: Push notification sent to the owner's smartphone if the vehicle's anti-theft alarm is triggered, enabling immediate awareness and response regardless of proximity.
Basic — Criterion: In-app push notification when vehicle alarm is triggered.
Medium — Criterion: Alarm notification with vehicle GPS location at time of trigger.
High — Criterion: Alarm notification with live camera snapshot and emergency contact integration.

Parameter Description: Telematics features allowing the owner to set geographic geofences, speed limits, and time-based access restrictions for the vehicle, receiving alerts via the app when boundaries are exceeded — useful for fleet and young-driver management.
Basic — Criterion: Speed alert notification when vehicle exceeds defined limit.
Medium — Criterion: Geo-fence boundary alert plus speed limit alert.
High — Criterion: Combined geo-fence, speed, and curfew time monitoring with automated event log and full alert customisation.
Optimised Parameter Name: Remote Geofence, Speed and Curfew Monitoring

Parameter Description: Specialised parked-vehicle modes. Pet Mode maintains safe cabin temperature for animals with an exterior screen notification. Camp Mode enables extended climate and power use. Work Mode activates power outlets and productivity features.
Basic — Criterion: Cabin temperature maintained for pet safety with exterior display notification.
Medium — Criterion: Pet mode + Camp mode with USB power enabled for extended stay.
High — Criterion: Pet, Camp, and Work modes with power management, app monitoring, and occupant safety interlock.
Optimised Parameter Name: Pet Mode / Camp Mode / Work Mode

Parameter Description: Remote live camera feed via the companion app, showing a view from the vehicle's interior camera(s) or 360-degree exterior surround cameras — enabling security checks or confirming vehicle surroundings remotely.
Basic — Criterion: Static image from one exterior camera viewable in app.
Medium — Criterion: Live exterior camera feed from one or more cameras in app.
High — Criterion: Live 360° exterior view and/or interior camera feed accessible remotely via app.
Optimised Parameter Name: Remote Live Camera View (Interior and 360 Exterior)

Category Description: Advanced Driver Assistance Systems handling longitudinal and lateral driving tasks while in motion: Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC), Lane Centering, Predictive Speed management, Hands-Free Level 2+, and Conditional Level 3 autonomy. These systems reduce driver fatigue on long journeys, contribute to safety ratings, and represent the primary axis of competitive differentiation toward autonomous driving capability.

Parameter: Assisted Driving L2 : ACC + lane centering / positioning
Parameter Description: The system can also provide steering assistance and suppress acceleration at low speeds to help prevent collisions
Optimised Parameter Name: Level 2 Assisted Driving (ACC with Lane Centring)

Parameter Description: System that reads road speed limit signs via camera and/or map data.
Basic — Criterion: Speed limit sign recognition displayed as information only.
Medium — Criterion: Detected speed limit proposed to driver who confirms via button to set ACC.
High — Criterion: Automatic speed adjustment to match detected road speed limit sign.

Parameter Description: Enhancement of standard ACC that uses map and real-time data to anticipate speed changes before sensors detect them.
Basic — Criterion: Standard ACC with fixed following distance — no predictive adjustment.
Medium — Criterion: Predictive speed reduction for upcoming speed limit changes and curves.
High — Criterion: Predictive speed management using map data: speed limits, curves, roundabouts, and intersections.

Parameter: Extended Auto Resume with leading vehicle after complete stop
Parameter Description: While using the ACC Stop & Go feature, the Extended Auto Resume function aims to increase the duration between the stop and go status.
Automated lane-change assistance on highways
Basic — Criterion: Driver-initiated: driver activates the turn indicator; system checks the target lane and executes the manoeuvre if the space is clear.
Medium — Criterion: Driver-confirmed: system detects that a lane change is relevant (to follow the planned route or overtake a slow vehicle), proposes it to the driver, and executes only after the driver confirms.
High — Criterion: System-initiated: system autonomously identifies the need to overtake a slow vehicle, proposes the manoeuvre, and — after driver confirmation — fully executes the lane change including return to the original lane.

Parameter Description: Route-following lane-change automation on highways.
Basic — Criterion: No assisted navigation lane changes — manual lane changes required.
Medium — Criterion: System proposes route-following lane change; driver must confirm to execute.
High — Criterion: System automatically initiates and executes route-following lane changes without driver confirmation.

Parameter: Turn Signal Activated Blind Spot View
Parameter Description: When the driver activates a turn indicator, the system displays a live camera feed of the corresponding adjacent lane/blind spot on the IVI screen or cluster, supplementing mirror visibility.
Basic — Criterion: Mirror-integrated BSM indicator light only.
Medium — Criterion: Camera view on IVI displayed when indicator activated — shows adjacent lane.
High — Criterion: Camera view in cluster (driver axis) when indicator activated with dynamic path overlay.
Optimised Parameter Name: Turn Signal Activated Blind Spot Camera View

Parameter Description: Hands-off driving capability (SAE Level 2+) where the driver can remove hands from the steering wheel while the system manages steering, speed, and lane keeping.
Basic — Criterion: L2 only — hands must remain on steering wheel at all times.
Medium — Criterion: Hands-free L2+ on motorways/controlled-access roads only.
High — Criterion: Hands-free L2+ on motorways AND secondary/rural roads.

Parameter Description: SAE Level 3 conditional automation: the system manages all driving tasks within defined operational conditions, and the driver can disengage attention (eyes off road) for extended periods.
Basic — Criterion: L3 conditional automation on motorways at up to 60 km/h (traffic-jam pilot).
Medium — Criterion: L3 on motorways up to 95 km/h.
High — Criterion: L3 on motorways above 95 km/h (or broader road-type coverage).

Parameter Description: Real-time graphical representation of the vehicle's perceived environment — surrounding vehicles, lane markings, pedestrians, cyclists, and other objects — displayed on the instrument cluster or IVI to give the driver a bird's-eye situational awareness view.
Basic — Criterion: Schematic vehicle icon with basic lane and lead-vehicle display.
Medium — Criterion: Real-time visualisation of lanes, detected vehicles, and road markings in cluster.
High — Criterion: Full 360° animated environment: lanes, vehicles, cyclists, pedestrians, and road furniture on a live 3D rendered map.

Parameter Description: The Euro NCAP (New Car Assessment Programme) overall safety star rating for the vehicle, reflecting performance across Adult Occupant, Child Occupant, Vulnerable Road User, and Safety Assist categories.
Basic — Criterion: 3-star Euro NCAP rating.
Medium — Criterion: 4-star Euro NCAP rating (or dual rating up to 4★).
High — Criterion: 5-star Euro NCAP rating (or dual rating up to 5★).

Parameter Description: IIHS (Insurance Institute for Highway Safety) recognition programme rating vehicle safety performance across crashworthiness and crash avoidance criteria in the USA.
Basic — Criterion: IIHS Good ratings across most categories — no TSP award.
Medium — Criterion: IIHS Top Safety Pick (TSP) award.
High — Criterion: IIHS Top Safety Pick+ (TSP+) award — highest IIHS designation.

Category Description: Systems that warn drivers of imminent hazards and actively intervene to prevent collisions: Blind Spot Monitoring and Assist, Rear Cross Traffic Alert and Braking, Safe Exit (Door Opening) Assist, and Night Vision. These features are heavily weighted in Euro NCAP and IIHS safety ratings, making them critical both for consumer safety outcomes and for the vehicle's market positioning.

Parameter Description: System monitoring the vehicle's blind-spot zones alongside and to the rear. Medium = visual/audio warning alerts when a vehicle is detected in the blind spot during a lane change attempt; High = warning plus active steering correction to physically resist the lane change.
Basic — Criterion: Visual BSM indicator in mirror or A-pillar — audio alert optional.
Medium — Criterion: Visual and audio warning preventing lane change when vehicle detected in blind spot.
High — Criterion: Warning plus active steering correction to resist lane change into occupied blind spot.

Parameter Description:
M: Alert of an obstacle approaching perpendicular to the vehicle path while in reverse 
H: Automatic braking to avoid an obstacle approaching perpendicular to the vehicle path while in reverse
Optimised Parameter Name: Rear Cross Traffic Alert and Braking

Parameter Description: Alerts the user of an approaching pedestrian, cyclist, or vehicle when opening the door

Infrared or thermal imaging system displayed on the IVI or cluster, enhancing the driver's ability to detect pedestrians, animals, and obstacles beyond the reach of the headlights in low-light or adverse weather conditions.
Basic — Criterion: Monochrome infrared front camera image on IVI (manual activation).
Medium — Criterion: Night vision with pedestrian and animal object highlighting on IVI.
High — Criterion: Night vision with pedestrian/animal classification, cluster pop-up alert, and high-beam integration.

Category Description: Technologies assisting with parking and slow-speed manoeuvres: proximity sensors, rear-view and 360-degree surround cameras, 3D bowl and transparent-chassis views, semi- and fully automated parking, remote parking via smartphone, and home-zone replay. As urban environments become denser and vehicles wider, parking assistance has become a baseline expectation across vehicle segments.

Parameter Description: M: Alerts when there are obstacles in front and behind vehicle during low/speed maneuvering and parking via audible and visual warnings / H: Alerts when there are obstacles in front, behind and alongside vehicle during low/speed maneuvering and parking via audible and visual warnings

Parameter: Rear View Camera including dynamic gridlines
Parameter Description: Rear-facing camera displayed on the IVI when reversing. Includes static parking guidelines and, at higher levels, dynamic guidelines that rotate in real time with the steering angle to show the predicted vehicle path.
Basic — Criterion: Static rear view camera image without guidelines.
Medium — Criterion: Rear view with static reference/parking guidelines.
High — Criterion: Dynamic steering-angle-linked guidelines showing predicted vehicle path and trailer hitch overlay.
Optimised Parameter Name: Rear View Camera with Dynamic Guidelines

Parameter Description: Four-camera stitched bird's-eye composite view of the vehicle and its immediate surroundings displayed at low speed, enabling precise manoeuvring in tight spaces. Includes selectable directional camera views for close obstacle inspection.
Basic — Criterion: Four-camera stitched top-down 360° view at low speed.
Medium — Criterion: 360° view with selectable individual directional camera angles.
High — Criterion: 360° view with 3D bowl perspective, transparent body overlay, and selectable zoom areas.

Parameter Description: Advanced variant of surround-view that renders a three-dimensional rotating perspective view of the vehicle and its surroundings, providing enhanced spatial depth perception compared to a flat top-down image.
Basic — Criterion: Flat top-down 360° composite view (no perspective elevation).
Medium — Criterion: Slightly elevated 3D perspective 360° view.
High — Criterion: Full interactive 3D bowl view with adjustable camera angle and vehicle transparency option.

Parameter Description: Composite camera view using front grille and side-mirror cameras to show the terrain and obstacles directly in front of and between the front wheels — a zone normally invisible to the driver — displayed on the central touchscreen.
Basic — Criterion: Front grille camera view showing terrain ahead of bumper.
Medium — Criterion: Front grille + mirror cameras showing terrain between front wheels.
High — Criterion: Full composite terrain view: front grille, mirrors, and ground clearance visualisation.

Parameter: Clear Path Under Body View - Transparent Chassis
Parameter Description: Advanced camera processing that creates a simulated under-vehicle or transparent-chassis view using delayed front and side camera feeds, helping drivers navigate obstacles and kerbs that would otherwise be hidden beneath the vehicle.
Basic — Criterion: Delayed single-camera front undercarriage approximation.
Medium — Criterion: Multi-camera composite simulating underbody view.
High — Criterion: Real-time transparent chassis rendering with camera interpolation and obstacle highlighting.

Parameter Description: System that handles the parking manoeuvre without sustained driver input. Medium = system controls steering only (driver manages throttle/brake) for parallel and perpendicular spaces; High = fully automated including steering, throttle, braking, and gear selection.
Basic — Criterion: Park Assist only — driver controls throttle/brake; system steers.
Medium — Criterion: Semi-automated: system controls steering for parallel and perpendicular spaces.
High — Criterion: Fully automated: system controls steering, throttle, braking, and gear selection — hands-free.

Parameter Description: System enabling the driver to control parking from outside the vehicle via a smartphone app. Medium = straight-line pull-in/pull-out only; High = full automated parallel and perpendicular parking with the driver supervising from outside.
Basic — Criterion: Remote control via app for straight-line pull-in/pull-out only.
Medium — Criterion: Remote parking for straight-line plus simple 90° spaces.
High — Criterion: Full remote automated parallel and perpendicular parking with driver supervising via app outside the vehicle.

Parameter Description: The vehicle records the driven path as the driver approaches a destination, then autonomously replicates the manoeuvre in reverse — useful for navigating narrow lanes, driveways, or complex approaches.
Basic — Criterion: Manual reverse guidance only — no path recording.
Medium — Criterion: System records and partially assists the reverse of the last driven path.
High — Criterion: System fully records driven path and autonomously replicates it in reverse with obstacle detection.

Parameter Description: The driver trains the vehicle on a specific repeated parking path (e.g., entering a home garage), which is stored and can subsequently be executed autonomously with a single command.
Basic — Criterion: Driver trains a simple home/garage path manually.
Medium — Criterion: System records and replays a single stored home-zone path.
High — Criterion: Multiple stored home-zone paths with automatic path selection and hands-free execution.

Parameter Description: SAE Level 4 automated valet parking capability: the vehicle can self-park in any compatible parking structure without a driver present or monitoring the manoeuvre.
Basic — Criterion: Remote parking limited to designated smart-parking facilities only.
Medium — Criterion: Automated valet in supported structures with limited supervision.
High — Criterion: Full SAE L4 automated valet in any compatible parking structure — no driver monitoring required.

Category Description: Dedicated systems for vehicles towing a trailer or caravan: extended 360° surround view, trailer reverse steering control, hitch alignment assistance, sway control, and hands-free L2+ while towing. A decisive differentiator for pick-up trucks, SUVs, and light commercial vehicles, and a primary reason buyers choose one truck brand over another in North America and growing global truck markets.

Parameter Description: Auxiliary trailer mounted cameras provide a constructed top view of the vehicle, trailer and surroundings to assist them while maneuvering and parking.  Extends top-down 360 surround view between vehicle and trailer.  in addition to a top-down view, Driver can select specific cameras to focus views on

System that decouples the trailer's direction from the steering wheel: the driver uses a dedicated dial or interface to select the desired trailer direction, while the system independently controls the vehicle's steering to achieve it — eliminating the counterintuitive reversal of steering input.
Basic — Criterion: Driver steers via normal wheel; cameras provide guidance only.
Medium — Criterion: Dial-based directional input with partial steering assistance for trailer reversing.
High — Criterion: Full TRSC: driver selects direction via dial; system fully controls steering to follow intended trailer path.

Parameter Description: Camera-based system that overlays dynamic predicted path lines on the rear camera image when reversing with a trailer, helping the driver judge the trailer's trajectory and alerting to risk of jack-knifing.
Basic — Criterion: Static camera view showing trailer position while reversing.
Medium — Criterion: Dynamic predicted path lines overlaid on rear camera image.
High — Criterion: Dynamic path prediction with jack-knife alert and real-time trailer angle indicator.
Optimised Parameter Name: Trailer Reverse Guidance with Path Overlay

Parameter Description: System that takes over steering while the driver manages throttle and brake, using rear cameras and algorithms to guide the vehicle's hitch ball precisely into alignment with the trailer coupler.
Basic — Criterion: Rear camera with static hitch target overlay only.
Medium — Criterion: Driver-guided alignment with camera and sensor distance indicators.
High — Criterion: Fully assisted: system controls steering to align hitch ball to coupler; driver manages throttle/brake only.

Parameter Description: Live camera feed from a camera mounted at the rear of the trailer, providing an unobstructed view behind the trailer during driving — equivalent to a conventional rear-view mirror but extended past the trailer's length.
Basic — Criterion: Camera port available; customer must supply and connect own camera.
Medium — Criterion: OEM-supplied auxiliary trailer camera with wired connection and basic head-unit display.
High — Criterion: Integrated trailer rear view in digital rear-view mirror with automatic activation while driving.

Parameter: Digital Rear View Mirror - Tow Mode
Parameter Description: Tow-specific mode for the digital rear-view mirror that simultaneously displays three camera feeds: left side-mirror view, right side-mirror view, and the trailer rear camera — giving the driver complete rear visibility despite the trailer blocking the conventional view.
Basic — Criterion: Digital rear-view mirror without tow-mode camera integration.
Medium — Criterion: DRVM with trailer camera feed as additional selectable view.
High — Criterion: DRVM tow mode: three simultaneous views — left mirror, right mirror, and trailer-rear camera.
Optimised Parameter Name: Digital Rear View Mirror Tow Mode

Parameter Description: Extension of the vehicle's L2 Lane Centring Assistance (LCA) to remain active and functional while a trailer is hitched, with adapted control algorithms accounting for the increased length and altered handling dynamics.
Basic — Criterion: Standard L2 lane centring without trailer detection or control adaptation.
Medium — Criterion: L2 lane centring active with trailer connected; adapted steering torque.
High — Criterion: Full L2 with trailer: adapted control algorithms accounting for trailer length, weight, and sway dynamics.

Parameter Description: Extension of hands-free Level 2+ driving capability to remain active and functional while towing a trailer, with trailer-aware control tuning. Represents a significant technical achievement given the added complexity of trailer dynamics.
Basic — Criterion: Hands-free L2+ not available when towing.
Medium — Criterion: Hands-free L2+ available while towing on motorways with speed limitation.
High — Criterion: Full hands-free L2+ while towing on motorways with trailer-specific control tuning.

Parameter Description: Enhanced electronic stability intervention specifically targeting trailer oscillation (sway): detects sway forces via sensors, and responds with coordinated stability control braking, trailer brake controller commands, and/or ePAS steering torque overlay to dampen the oscillation before it escalates.
Basic — Criterion: Basic electronic stability control applied reactively when sway is detected.
Medium — Criterion: Dedicated sway detection with automatic engine torque reduction and brake application.
High — Criterion: Enhanced sway control: stability control + trailer brake controller + ePAS steering torque overlay.

Category Description: All physical and digital elements forming the driver and occupant environment: display hardware (cluster, central screen, HUD, passenger screen, rear screens), display technology and integration, interaction modalities (touch, voice, gesture, gaze), interior and exterior lighting design, seating comfort and adjustment, door mechanisms, and personalisation systems. The cockpit domain defines premium perception, ergonomic quality, and the first emotional impression at point of sale.

Parameter Description: Size of the fully digital instrument cluster display.
Basic — Criterion: Fully digital cluster smaller than 10" (or focused display < 14.9").
Medium — Criterion: Digital cluster 10"–12" (or focused display ≥ 14.9").
High — Criterion: Digital cluster larger than 12".

Parameter Description: Size of the primary central infotainment touchscreen.
Basic — Criterion: Central display smaller than 12" (or focused display < 14.9").
Medium — Criterion: Central display 12"–14" (or focused display ≥ 14.9").
High — Criterion: Central display larger than 14".

Parameter Description: Panel technology used for the vehicle's primary displays.
Basic — Criterion: TFT LCD display technology.
Medium — Criterion: OLED or Full-Array Local Dimming (FALD) LCD.
High — Criterion: Micro-LED, laser projection, transparent, or holographic display technology.

Parameter:
Specific shape of screen
High : specific shape, curved
Parameter Description: Describes whether the vehicle's displays use a custom or non-rectangular shape, curved form, or distinctive design signature beyond standard flat rectangular panels — a differentiator for premium brand identity.
Basic — Criterion: Standard rectangular flat panel display.
Medium — Criterion: Custom-shaped or mildly curved display.
High — Criterion: Distinctively shaped or deeply curved display as a brand design signature.

Parameter Description: The degree to which multiple displays are integrated into a unified physical and visual design.
Basic — Criterion: Multiple displays behind a single glass cover panel.
Medium — Criterion: Seamlessly integrated displays with minimal bezel separation.
High — Criterion: Advanced integration: reflective (CRU/Phantom) or fully hidden/retractable screen concept.

Parameter Description: Capability for the central display to physically move its position.
Basic — Criterion: Fixed screen — no movement capability.
Medium — Criterion: Rotatable screen (pivot axis adjustment).
High — Criterion: Sliding along a track or foldable/retractable screen.

Parameter Description: Dedicated display for the front passenger providing independent content viewing (video, navigation, apps) without interfering with the driver's view.
Basic — Criterion: No dedicated front passenger display.
Medium — Criterion: Front passenger screen smaller than 12".
High — Criterion: Front passenger screen 12" or larger with independent content capability.

Parameter Description: Rear-seat entertainment screen solution.
Basic — Criterion: Tablet holder/mount for customer's own device.
Medium — Criterion: Two OEM-embedded rear displays (headrest or ceiling-mounted).
High — Criterion: Single panoramic OEM-embedded rear screen spanning the full rear cabin.

Parameter Description: Dedicated touchscreen for rear-seat passengers to independently control climate (HVAC), seat heating/cooling/massage, and other comfort functions without requiring front-seat occupant involvement.
Basic — Criterion: Physical climate controls for rear occupants only — no screen.
Medium — Criterion: Basic rear touchscreen for climate control only.
High — Criterion: Rear comfort touchscreen controlling HVAC, seat heating/cooling/massage, and entertainment.

Parameter Description: A Head-Up Display (HUD) projects driving-relevant information directly onto the windshield or a combiner panel within the driver's natural line of sight, reducing the need to look away from the road.
Basic — Criterion: Standard windshield or combiner HUD projecting speed, basic navigation arrows, and warning icons at approximately 2 metres virtual distance in the driver's line of sight.
Medium — Criterion: Extended 2.5D HUD projecting speed, navigation guidance, speed limit, and lane information at a virtual distance of 4.5 to 10 metres, providing a greater sense of depth and reduced eye refocus effort.
High — Criterion: Augmented Reality HUD projecting information at more than 10 metres virtual distance, with navigation arrows and hazard highlights spatially anchored to the actual road geometry and lane markings ahead of the vehicle.

Parameter Description: Dashboard-integrated smartphone mounting solution.
Basic — Criterion: Physical holder only — no charging capability.
Medium — Criterion: Dashboard holder with wired USB charging.
High — Criterion: Dashboard holder with integrated Qi wireless charging.

Parameter Description: Number of simultaneous wireless (Qi) charging pads in the cabin.
Basic — Criterion: One wireless Qi charging pad in the cabin.
Medium — Criterion: Two wireless charging pads.
High — Criterion: Three or more wireless charging pads.

Parameter Description: Rear-view mirror that can display a live camera feed from a rear-mounted camera instead of (or in addition to) the conventional reflected view.
Basic — Criterion: Conventional glass mirror only — no digital mode.
Medium — Criterion: Digital-display mode only (no conventional mirror fallback).
High — Criterion: Dual-mode: switchable between conventional glass mirror and digital display.

Parameter Description: New type of modalities to interact : gesture commandes, looked to stg fixed for example
Optimised Parameter Name: Advanced Interaction Modalities (Gesture and Gaze Control)

Parameter Description: Extent to which the driver can personalise the layout and content of the HMI across multiple screens — home page widget arrangement, cluster data views, HUD information fields — and save configurations to a profile.
Basic — Criterion: Fixed HMI layout — no user customisation.
Medium — Criterion: Customisable home screen and cluster widgets (limited options).
High — Criterion: Full layout customisation across home screen, cluster, and HUD with saveable themed profiles.

Parameter Description: In-vehicle personalisation of physical comfort settings including smart seat adjustment and memory, ergonomic preferences, HMI content favourites, and stored personal settings accessible without cloud connectivity.
Basic — Criterion: Manual seat position only — no memory function.
Medium — Criterion: Electric seat with single one-touch memory position.
High — Criterion: Smart seat with personalised ergonomic profile, HMI content favourites, and OTA preference updates.
Optimised Parameter Name: On-Board Driver Personalisation (Smart Seat and Ergonomics)

Parameter: Automatic Personalization (Digital setting) by SD or others
Parameter Description: automatic recognition of customer by mobile or digital key and application of linked profile settings
Optimised Parameter Name: Automatic Profile Loading via Digital Key Recognition

Parameter Description: Dynamic headlight system that adjusts beam direction and pattern in response to steering angle, vehicle speed, and road type to continuously optimise road illumination in curves, junctions, and varying speed conditions.
Basic — Criterion: Fixed headlight beam — no directional adaptation.
Medium — Criterion: Cornering lights (static or swivelling) active at low speed only.
High — Criterion: Full dynamic AFS: continuous beam steering linked to steering angle, speed, and road/map data.

Parameter Description: Advanced high-beam system that maintains full high-beam illumination at all times while selectively masking specific zones around detected oncoming vehicles and preceding traffic, eliminating dazzle without reducing overall road illumination.
Basic — Criterion: Manual high beam only — no auto anti-glare functionality.
Medium — Criterion: Automatic high-beam assist (switches between high and dipped beam).
High — Criterion: Glare-free high beam: continuously active high beam with selective masking of oncoming/preceding vehicles.

Parameter Description: Technology used for the vehicle's exterior lights (headlights and tail lights).
Basic — Criterion: LED exterior lighting (headlights, tail lights).
Medium — Criterion: Matrix LED: independently switchable LED groups for basic beam shaping.
High — Criterion: Pixel LED: individually addressable pixels for precise beam control and animated light signatures.

Parameter Description: Ambient interior lighting system that illuminates the cabin surfaces, footwells, door panels, and dashboard contours to create atmosphere and enhance the perceived quality of the interior.
Basic — Criterion: Fixed single-colour ambient lighting illuminating the cabin with one static colour tone that cannot be changed by the driver.
Medium — Criterion: Multi-colour RGB ambient lighting system allowing the driver to select from a palette of colours or preset themes to personalise the cabin atmosphere.
High — Criterion: Functional ambient lighting that dynamically changes colour and intensity in real time in response to vehicle events such as navigation turn instructions, ADAS alerts, incoming calls, door opening warnings, and active drive mode changes

Parameter Description: Preset or customisable interior atmosphere packages combining ambient lighting, sound, climate, and seat settings.
Basic — Criterion: Fixed OEM-defined preset ambience themes.
Medium — Criterion: User-customisable ambience settings (colour, intensity, zone selection).
High — Criterion: Adaptive ambience responding to external triggers: weather, time of day, drive mode, or detected events.

Parameter Description: The brand emblem positioned at the centre of the steering wheel is backlit, transforming a standard badge into an illuminated design signature.
Basic — Criterion: Monochromatic backlit emblem (single colour).
Medium — Criterion: Monochromatic backlit emblem (single colour).
High — Criterion: Full RGB illuminated emblem dynamically linked to drive mode or ambient lighting theme.
Optimised Parameter Name: Illuminated Brand Emblem on Steering Wheel

Parameter Description: Power-adjustable seating allowing occupants to electrically control seat position, recline, lumbar support, and other ergonomic parameters via buttons or a touchscreen interface rather than manual mechanical levers.
Basic — Criterion: Electric adjustment is available on the driver seat only, allowing the driver to set and modify their seating position via powered controls.
Medium — Criterion: Electric adjustment is available on both the driver and front passenger seats, providing powered comfort control for all front occupants.
High — Criterion: Electric adjustment is available across all seats including the rear, providing powered comfort control for every occupant in the vehicle.

Parameter Description: Memory function for electric seat (and optionally mirror/steering column) positions, allowing multiple drivers to save and instantly recall their preferred ergonomic settings.
Basic — Criterion: No memory function — manual repositioning required each time.
Medium — Criterion: Single memory position per driver.
High — Criterion: Multiple memory positions per driver, automatically recalled via personalisation profile.

Parameter Description: In-seat pneumatic or mechanical massage system using inflatable air chambers or vibrating elements integrated into the seat structure to provide comfort and reduce fatigue during long journeys.
Basic — Criterion: Massage function is available on the driver seat only, providing fatigue relief and comfort support for the driver during long journeys.
Medium — Criterion: Massage function is available on both the driver and front passenger seats, extending active comfort support to all front occupants simultaneously.
High — Criterion: Massage function is available across all seats including the rear, providing full cabin occupant comfort and wellbeing support for every passenger on board.

Parameter Description: Active seat ventilation using fans to draw air through the seat cushion, providing cooling comfort.
Basic — Criterion: Active ventilation for front seats only.
Medium — Criterion: Front seat ventilation with multi-level fan speed control.
High — Criterion: Active ventilation for front and rear seats.

Parameter Description: Electrically operated interior door release — replacing a conventional mechanical handle with a button, capacitive sensor, or fully electronic latch system for a cleaner interior design.
Basic — Criterion: Conventional mechanical interior door handle only.
Medium — Criterion: Electric interior door release (button or capacitive touch sensor).
High — Criterion: Fully electric interior door with soft-close, auto-hold, and power operation.

Parameter Description: Motorised door system replacing conventional manual door operation with powered automatic open and close mechanisms, activated via button, handle sensor, or proximity detection.
Basic — Criterion: Motorised automatic open and close is available on the front doors only, providing powered entry and exit convenience for the driver and front passenger.
Medium — Criterion: Motorised automatic open and close is available on the front doors with additional features such as speed-sensitive closing force, obstacle detection, or programmable opening angle.
High — Criterion: Motorised automatic open and close is available on both front and rear doors, providing fully powered entry and exit convenience for all occupants across the entire vehicle.

Parameter: Driver space release (Linked to L3/L4 autonomous vehicle)
Parameter Description: When L3/L4 autonomous mode is engaged, the steering wheel automatically retracts and/or the driver's seat reclines, releasing space in the driver's zone for a more comfortable resting posture during autonomous operation.
Basic — Criterion: Fixed steering column — no retraction capability.
Medium — Criterion: Telescopically retractable steering wheel when L3 is engaged.
High — Criterion: Full driver-space release: steering wheel retraction plus seat recline when L3/L4 autonomous mode is active.
Optimised Parameter Name: Driver Space Release for Autonomous Mode (Retractable Steering Wheel)

Parameter Description: Motorised boot/tailgate operation.
Basic — Criterion: Manual tailgate — no powered operation.
Medium — Criterion: Powered tailgate (button-operated open/close).
High — Criterion: Hands-free powered tailgate (kick-sensor or proximity detection activation).

Parameter Description: The vehicle can be started and operated without a traditional push-button ignition — simply entering the vehicle and engaging drive gear initiates operation, streamlining the boarding experience.
Basic — Criterion: Conventional push-button start/stop required.
Medium — Criterion: Walk-up start: vehicle powers on when keyless entry is detected on approach.
High — Criterion: Buttonless operation: vehicle activates automatically on entry; gear engagement initiates motion.

Parameter Description: USB-C charging socket with elevated power delivery for fast device charging.
Basic — Criterion: Standard USB-A or USB-C up to 18 W.
Medium — Criterion: High-power USB-C at 45 W.
High — Criterion: Ultra-high-power USB-C at 60 W or above.

Parameter: Steer by wire with specific steering wheel shape
Parameter Description: Steer-by-wire (SbW) is a technology that replaces the conventional mechanical linkage between the steering wheel and the front wheels with an entirely electronic signal pathway, transmitting steering inputs via sensors, actuators, and control units.
Basic — Criterion: Conventional mechanical steering column connecting the steering wheel directly to the front wheels with no steer-by-wire technology present.
Medium — Criterion: Electro-mechanical steering system with a reduced or collapsible column architecture, representing a transitional step toward full steer-by-wire but retaining a mechanical fallback connection.
High — Criterion: Full steer-by-wire system with no mechanical connection between the steering wheel and the front axle, enabling a custom-shaped or non-circular steering wheel design and fully variable electronic steering ratio.
Optimised Parameter Name: Steer-by-Wire with Custom Steering Wheel Design

Category Description: Specialised equipment engineered for pickup truck utility, off-road adventure, and work-site productivity: trailer monitoring (tyre pressure, lights, tongue weight, health), bed utility (extenders, workbench tailgate, scales), auxiliary camera systems, onboard power generation, and mobile-office setups. These features address the professional and recreational demands of truck buyers and represent a critical differentiator in the highly competitive North American full-size and mid-size truck segments.

Parameter Description: System monitoring the tyre pressures of a hitched trailer and displaying individual readings inside the truck cab, alerting the driver to low-pressure conditions before they become safety issues.
Basic — Criterion: TPMS warning indicator when pressure is critically low only.
Medium — Criterion: Actual pressure readout per trailer axle displayed in cabin.
High — Criterion: Real-time per-wheel trailer tyre pressure and temperature with historical trend logging.

Parameter Description: Automated system that checks the functionality of all trailer lights at vehicle start-up or connection, alerting the driver to any burned-out bulbs before departure.
Basic — Criterion: Manual trailer lighting check via dashboard prompt.
Medium — Criterion: Automatic bulb check at ignition on with pass/fail indicator.
High — Criterion: Continuous trailer light monitoring with individual bulb identification and mobile app notification.

Parameter Description: Anti-theft system that detects if the trailer is disconnected from the hitch while the truck is unattended, triggering the vehicle alarm and/or sending a push notification to the owner's mobile app.
Basic — Criterion: Vehicle alarm triggered if trailer coupling is disturbed.
Medium — Criterion: Vehicle alarm plus in-cabin notification when trailer is disconnected.
High — Criterion: Alarm plus push notification to mobile app with GPS location at time of disconnect.
Optimised Parameter Name: Trailer Theft Alert and Disconnect Notification

Parameter: Payload Indicator w/ Exterior Readout / Onboard Scales
Parameter Description: Onboard weighing system using suspension sensors to calculate and display the current payload in the truck bed in real time — showing weight, percentage of safe load capacity, and triggering overload warnings both inside the cabin and (at higher levels) on an exterior display at the tailgate.
Basic — Criterion: Estimated payload percentage indicator in cluster only.
Medium — Criterion: Digital payload weight display in cabin with overload warning.
High — Criterion: Real-time payload scale on interior display and tailgate exterior panel with overload alert.
Optimised Parameter Name: Payload Indicator and Onboard Bed Scales

Parameter Description: System measuring the static tongue weight (downward force of trailer on hitch ball) to help the driver optimise weight distribution during loading and confirm safe loading before departure.
Basic — Criterion: Tongue weight guidance based on trailer profile entry (estimated only).
Medium — Criterion: Measured tongue weight with acceptable range guidance on central display.
High — Criterion: Measured tongue weight for conventional and 5th-wheel/gooseneck hitches with weight-distribution guidance and app support.
Optimised Parameter Name: Trailer Tongue Weight Measurement and Guidance

Parameter Description: Tailgate designed and equipped to function as a job-site work surface, with features such as a flat stable work area, integrated rulers, clamp pockets, tool holders, tie-down hooks, cup holders, and USB power outlets.
Basic — Criterion: Flat tailgate surface usable as basic work area only.
Medium — Criterion: Tailgate with integrated ruler, tie-down hooks, and cup holders.
High — Criterion: Full workbench: flat stable surface, clamp pockets, tool holders, cup holders, and USB power outlets.

Parameter Description: Camera and sensor system that maintains full functionality of the rear-view and 360-degree surround cameras when the tailgate is lowered — adjusting camera angles automatically and keeping backup sensors active.
Basic — Criterion: Separate rear camera available when tailgate is down.
Medium — Criterion: Automatic camera angle adjustment maintaining rear view with tailgate lowered.
High — Criterion: Full continuity: adaptive camera, functioning 360° view, and backup sensors integrated into tailgate.

Parameter Description: Auxiliary cameras with their own battery power source that connect wirelessly to the truck's infotainment head unit, allowing flexible mounting anywhere on the truck or trailer without cable routing.
Basic — Criterion: One wireless auxiliary camera with limited mounting options.
Medium — Criterion: Up to two wireless cameras with dedicated battery power source.
High — Criterion: Multiple wireless cameras mountable anywhere on truck or trailer with automatic head-unit integration.

Parameter Description: Auxiliary cameras hardwired to the truck's electrical system for a permanent, reliable connection — mountable at various positions on the truck or trailer for expanded visibility coverage.
Basic — Criterion: Single additional wired auxiliary camera.
Medium — Criterion: Up to two wired cameras integrated into the vehicle wiring harness.
High — Criterion: Full auxiliary wired camera system with multiple inputs and configurable display zones.

Parameter Description: A retractable panel (typically stored beneath the bed floor) that slides or folds out beyond the tailgate to increase the effective cargo bed length for oversized loads.
Basic — Criterion: Fixed rigid bed extender accessory — no retraction.
Medium — Criterion: Slide-out bed extender stored beneath the bed floor.
High — Criterion: Powered or assisted slide-out bed extender with automatic deployment.

Parameter Description: Tailgate can be divided into swing-out doors that hinge 60/40 split. A kick out step allows quick access to the truck's bed and there is no interference with the trailer hitch.

A removable or fold-down panel forming the rear wall of the cab, separating the cabin from the truck bed. When opened, it extends the usable cargo length through the cab interior for carrying exceptionally long items.
Basic — Criterion: Fixed cab rear wall — no midgate access.
Medium — Criterion: Removable midgate panel to extend bed into cab area.
High — Criterion: Folding/retractable midgate with full pass-through to front cab for extra-long cargo.

Parameter Description: Comprehensive telematics system monitoring the overall health status of a connected trailer from inside the truck: tyre pressures, temperatures, battery voltage, lighting status, vibration, and optionally interior cargo temperature.
Basic — Criterion: Trailer connection status and lights check only.
Medium — Criterion: Tyre pressure and battery voltage monitoring for connected trailer.
High — Criterion: Comprehensive: tyre pressure, temperature, vibration, lights, battery voltage, and optional interior temp monitoring.

Parameter Description: Camera mounted in the truck bed area providing a view of the cargo and bed contents — useful for monitoring load security while driving and for general bed use.
Basic — Criterion: Fixed rear-facing camera in bed showing cargo area.
Medium — Criterion: Wide-angle bed camera with night vision capability.
High — Criterion: 360° or multi-angle bed camera with motion detection and remote viewing via companion app.

Parameter Description: Integrated infotainment application serving as a unified command centre for all towing operations: trailer profiles and setup, real-time trailer health data, maintenance schedules, camera feeds, and towing guidance.
Basic — Criterion: Basic trailer checklist or setup guide in infotainment.
Medium — Criterion: Trailer management app with profile storage and key telemetry data.
High — Criterion: Full hub: profiles, health monitor, maintenance schedule, camera feeds, and hitch guidance in one integrated interface.

Parameter Description: Onboard electrical power generation providing AC outlets and/or high-power DC sockets for operating tools, appliances, or charging equipment directly from the truck — without a separate generator.
Basic — Criterion: One 12 V accessory outlet or standard socket up to 400 W.
Medium — Criterion: Multiple AC sockets (≥2) up to 1.8 kW total output.
High — Criterion: High-power onboard generator (≥2.4 kW AC) with multiple outlets for professional power tools.

Parameter Description: Interior cab transformation capability converting the centre console area into a functional work surface — with a fold-out flat desk, USB power, device mounts, and storage — enabling productive remote work from inside the truck.
Basic — Criterion: Flat centre console surface usable informally as work area.
Medium — Criterion: Fold-out centre console work surface with USB charging.
High — Criterion: Full mobile office: fold-out table, document storage, monitor mount, power outlets, and Wi-Fi hotspot integration.
